text: The robotic-assisted right middle lobectomy involved robotic ports traversing along the intercostal space between the eighth and ninth intercostal space.
A single access port was used for an assistant and a suction device.

The most anterior structure for a right middle lobe would be the pulmonary vein.
The most posterior structure in a right middle lobe will be the pulmonary artery, and the bronchus is in the middle.

This is a slightly different pattern of the order that you would take things, given that most of the time for upper lobes you would proceed from the front to the back, taking the vein, then the artery, then the bronchus.
In the right middle lobe, you will often go from the front to the back and take the vein followed by the bronchus followed by the artery as we did in this case.
Branches of the artery are important to identify.

It is important to recognize visual evidence of tension on structures so that without the haptic feedback of the robot, visual evidence of tension on structures is a sign that injury may be occurring.
